event: A scene of the Exodus with women and children resting
symbols: resting women and children — the vulnerable and weary amid the Exodus journey
nursing mother — life sustained even in the wilderness of wandering
walking staffs — the pilgrimage through the desert toward the Promised Land
distant crowd — the vast multitude of Israel on the move
description: A grey-toned (grisaille) drawing showing a foreground group of women, children, and men resting under trees. A central woman nurses an infant while others sit or recline around her. A man at right leans on a walking staff. A dog sits nearby. In the middle ground and background, a large crowd of figures moves through a landscape with trees and distant buildings. The drawing is rendered in grey wash and white heightening on toned paper, with a watermark or stamp visible. The mood is one of exhaustion and brief respite during a long journey.
theological_description: Van Noort depicts a moment of rest during the Exodus, the great migration of Israel from Egypt to the Promised Land. The Book of Exodus and Numbers record the arduous forty-year journey through the wilderness with the entire community — men, women, children, and livestock (Exodus 12:37-38). The nursing mother at center recalls God's tender provision for his people even in the desert: 'Can a mother forget the baby at her breast? Though she may forget, I will not forget you' (Isaiah 49:15). The weariness of the travelers reflects the repeated complaints of the Israelites during the journey (Exodus 16:2-3, Numbers 11:4-6), yet also God's faithfulness in sustaining them: 'During the forty years that I led you through the wilderness, your clothes did not wear out, nor did the sandals on your feet' (Deuteronomy 29:5). The vast crowd in the background evokes the multitude who left Egypt — about 600,000 men on foot besides women and children (Exodus 12:37). This scene of rest amid the journey becomes a type of the pilgrim Church resting in hope during its earthly sojourn toward the heavenly promised land (Hebrews 4:9-11).

scripture_refs.reason: The Israelites set out with about 600,000 men plus women, children, and livestock
God sustained the people through forty years of wilderness wandering
A mother nursing her child — God's unforgetting care for his people
A Sabbath rest remains for the people of God — the journey toward ultimate rest
